author: dildick
body:
#### PR description:

* Last week we took at long run, 115k cosmic events, with the B904 GEM-CSC test stand. I analyzed the CSC trigger primitives. I determined that there was a minor disagreement between firmware and emulation for the CLCT algorithm. Disagreements for ALCTs and CLCTs are now at the same level: about 1 in 17,000 to 20,0000 trigger primitives is off. (Although this is with cosmics.) The CCLUT code calculation is well understood. There is just 1 difference in 119,000 CLCTs. In all, there are just  7 CLCTs different. Those may be worth investigating further. Most likely, there are small differences in other parts of the CLCT algorithm (other than CCLUT).
* A `PulseArray` class was introduced, largely to hide the 2D array and some of the trigger logic. This should not change anything.
* A redundant ghost cancellation function was removed
* A CLCT median timing logic was removed -  we cannot implement this in firmware anyway.
* Pieces of the logic in the findCLCT function or pretrigger function were put in separate functions - to make it easier to understand and maintain.

#### PR validation:

Tested so far on the cosmic data. 

Results from short run 10k events:
[CSC_dataVsEmul_B904_Cosmic_Run_210609_142357_ArrangePatFinderAndCCLUT.pdf](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/files/6789029/CSC_dataVsEmul_B904_Cosmic_Run_210609_142357_ArrangePatFinderAndCCLUT.pdf)

Results from long run 115k events:
[CSC_dataVsEmul_B904_Cosmic_Run_210701_135109.pdf](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/files/6789033/CSC_dataVsEmul_B904_Cosmic_Run_210701_135109.pdf)

I will test it on 25k Run-2 data events tomorrow and attach the plots. I do not expect any differences compared to https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/34245

@tahuang1991
title: Improvements to CSC trigger primitives following long cosmic run at B904 (CCLUT-16)
